VAL-NOVA-002.evidence: taxonomy.py defines all 6 dark pattern categories (manipulative_design, deceptive_content, coercive_flow, obstruction, sneaking, social_proof_manipulation), 6 audit scenarios (cookie_consent, subscription_cancellation, checkout_flow, account_deletion, newsletter_signup, pricing_comparison), 4 severity levels (low, medium, high, critical), and 3 personas (privacy_sensitive, cost_sensitive, exit_intent). Key modules (schemas/audit.py, providers/nova_act_browser.py, detectors/rule_engine.py, plus 3 test files) import from app.core.taxonomy. Dark pattern category strings are NOT hardcoded outside taxonomy.py in the app/ directory. Some scenario and legacy pattern_family strings are used inline in operational logic (rule_engine.py conditionals, audit_orchestrator.py templates) but the canonical definitions live in taxonomy.py and types (ScenarioType, PersonaType, DarkPatternCategory) are imported where used for type checking.
VAL-NOVA-002.details: Full evidence at evidence/nova-act-core/code-structure/VAL-NOVA-002-taxonomy-contents.txt. Taxonomy also includes regulatory mappings (FTC, GDPR, DSA, CPRA), evidence types, confidence thresholds, scenario-to-category mappings, and legacy pattern family backward compatibility. 6 files import from taxonomy: schemas/audit.py, providers/nova_act_browser.py, detectors/rule_engine.py, tests/test_taxonomy.py, tests/test_nova_act_provider.py, tests/test_audit_lifecycle.py.

VAL-NOVA-011.evidence: nova_act_browser.py defines 7 Pydantic BaseModel subclasses for structured extraction: CookieConsentObservation, CheckoutObservation, SubscriptionCancellationObservation, AccountDeletionObservation, NewsletterSignupObservation, PricingComparisonObservation, and ScreenshotEvidence. 13 act_get() calls found across all 6 scenario methods, each passing a schema parameter via .model_json_schema(). Extracted data is validated with model_validate() and mapped into JourneyObservation dataclass (from schemas/runtime.py). Both BOOL_SCHEMA for simple checks and full Pydantic JSON schemas for complex structured extraction are used.
VAL-NOVA-011.details: Full evidence at evidence/nova-act-core/code-structure/VAL-NOVA-011-act-get-evidence.txt. act_get calls at lines: 359, 369, 382, 434, 578, 607, 708, 743, 855, 884, 995, 1125, 1154. Each scenario uses act_get with its corresponding Pydantic model schema. Data flows: act_get → parsed_response → model_validate → ObservationEvidence fields → JourneyObservation. The pattern is consistent across all 6 scenarios (cookie_consent, checkout_flow, subscription_cancellation, account_deletion, newsletter_signup, pricing_comparison).

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-NOVA-002 confirmed taxonomy.py as single source of truth with all required definitions (6 categories, 6 scenarios, 4 severities, 3 personas) and proper imports across modules. VAL-NOVA-011 confirmed act_get() uses Pydantic schemas for structured extraction across all 6 scenarios with 7 BaseModel classes and 13 act_get() calls.
